title: Tonys Baby

jokes.text: No, no, no, no, I ran eight miles. He drove alongside in his cab.
jokes.setup: Two people appear to have exercised together, running 8 miles
jokes.punchline: Only one actually ran - the other just drove his cab alongside
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with efficient setup and clear punchline. Reveals character laziness in unexpected way.

dialogue.speaker: Cab Driver
dialogue.line: We did it! Eight miles!

dialogue.speaker: Runner
dialogue.line: You did it? You drove your cab alongside me the whole time!

dialogue.speaker: Cab Driver
dialogue.line: Well, my feet hurt too.

jokes.text: Well, the heater was stuck. It was like an oven in there. My body can't take that kind of strain.
jokes.setup: Cab driver explaining why driving alongside a runner was difficult
jokes.punchline: Claims sitting in a heated car was physically straining
jokes.explanation: Character-based humor showing extreme laziness and self-delusion about physical exertion.

dialogue.speaker: Cab Driver
dialogue.line: You try sitting in a heated car for three miles. My legs were cramping, my back was killing me, and I couldn't even open the door without losing my place!

jokes.text: It has removed more teeth than trench mouth.
jokes.setup: Reading about a boxer's devastating left hand
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to a dental disease for comedic effect
jokes.explanation: Dark but clever comparison that's both specific and unexpected. Good economy.

dialogue.speaker: Reader
dialogue.line: This boxer has a devastating left hand. Doctors say it's like periodontitis.

jokes.text: Yeah, 'Gums Banta.'
jokes.setup: Tony saying he could make a name for himself boxing
jokes.punchline: Louie suggests his name will be about losing his teeth
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie line - cynical, cutting, and economical. Character-specific pessimism.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: I'm telling you, I could make a name for myself boxing.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Yeah, and that name is gonna be 'Gums'.

jokes.text: You mean, with a color photo in Boxing Monthly?
jokes.setup: Someone asking if Tony wants to end up disfigured like the last fighter
jokes.punchline: Tony focuses on the prestige of being featured in the magazine
jokes.explanation: Shows Tony's oblivious optimism and misplaced priorities perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Disfigured? Are you kidding? Did you see the spread they gave me in Ring Magazine? They called me 'The Reverend.' I'm gonna be famous!

jokes.text: Jim, that's his mouth.
jokes.setup: Jim commenting on stitches needed to close a gap in photo
jokes.punchline: Revelation that what Jim thought was a wound is actually the fighter's mouth
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual comedy moment with perfect timing. Jim's confusion creates great payoff.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Look at this wound. He's gonna need at least twenty stitches.

dialogue.speaker: Someone
dialogue.line: That's not a wound, Jim. That's his mouth.

jokes.text: Well, so is Lupus. You don't see him wasting company time.
jokes.setup: Tony saying he needs to shower because he's sweaty and smelly
jokes.punchline: Louie points out another driver is also smelly but doesn't waste time
jokes.explanation: Classic Louie insult disguised as workplace efficiency. Character-appropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Yeah, well Lupus hasn't showered in three weeks and he's not complaining.

dialogue.speaker: Lupus
dialogue.line: I'm not complaining because I'm not here.

jokes.text: No matter where we are, it could be the crummiest, sleaziest joint, she fits right in.
jokes.setup: Tony describing what's special about his girlfriend Vicki
jokes.punchline: Her 'special quality' is fitting into horrible places
jokes.explanation: Tony's oblivious backhanded compliment reveals both characters perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: She's got this special quality. She can fit into horrible places.

jokes.text: Go on! Go on!
jokes.setup: Lupus receiving the air freshener gift
jokes.punchline: Lupus's enthusiastic reaction suggests he knows he needs it
jokes.explanation: Physical reaction beat showing character's lack of self-awareness about his odor.

dialogue.speaker: Louie De Palma
dialogue.line: Here, Lupus, I got you something.

dialogue.speaker: Lupus
dialogue.line: An air freshener? For me?

dialogue.speaker: Louie De Palma
dialogue.line: Yeah, I thought your cab could use it.

dialogue.speaker: Lupus
dialogue.line: Oh, thanks, Louie! I really appreciate it!

jokes.text: You're gonna go see your mother first. Oh, Vicki. No, Tony, I missed my period.
jokes.setup: Tony continuing to misunderstand what 'late' means
jokes.punchline: Vicki has to explicitly explain she missed her period
jokes.explanation: Extended misdirection payoff with clear character work and escalating confusion.

dialogue.speaker: Vicki
dialogue.line: Tony, I'm late.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Late? You're not late. We didn't have an appointment.

dialogue.speaker: Vicki
dialogue.line: No, Tony. I'm late. My period is late.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Oh! Oh... OH!

jokes.text: I just go and put on a little perfume.
jokes.setup: Tony thinking Vicki's bathroom trips were contraceptive
jokes.punchline: She was just putting on perfume
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline revealing Tony's misconception about romance and protection.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: You mean all this time you weren't using the bathroom for... contraception?

dialogue.speaker: Vicki
dialogue.line: No, Tony. I was just putting on perfume.

jokes.text: Gee, I guess nothing's foolproof, huh?
jokes.setup: Learning Vicki was just putting on perfume, not using contraception
jokes.punchline: Tony still thinks perfume was supposed to prevent pregnancy
jokes.explanation: Tony's continued obliviousness creates perfect character-driven humor.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Wait, so the perfume... it doesn't prevent pregnancy?

dialogue.speaker: Vicki
dialogue.line: No, Tony, it's just perfume.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: But I thought... I mean, you said you were taking precautions...

dialogue.speaker: Vicki
dialogue.line: I was! I was wearing perfume!

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Yeah, but how does that work exactly? Do you spray it and the baby just... doesn't happen?

jokes.text: No. You've already done your part.
jokes.setup: Tony offering to go to the doctor with Vicki
jokes.punchline: Vicki's dry response about his 'contribution'
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan delivery with perfect economy. Cuts through Tony's obliviousness.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: I'll go to the doctor with you.

dialogue.speaker: Vicki
dialogue.line: That's very sweet, but what exactly would be your contribution?

jokes.text: Yeah, I wanna drive, man. Give me a cab. Sorry, all out.
jokes.setup: Tony enthusiastically asking for a cab after Louie's pep talk
jokes.punchline: Louie immediately deflates him by saying there are no cabs
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie move - gives motivational speech then crushes hope immediately. Great timing.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Okay, I'm ready! Give me a cab!

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: There are no cabs.

jokes.text: Has anyone ever noticed how Tony eats? He's got the table manners of a Hun.
jokes.setup: Jim pretending Tony isn't there by talking about him
jokes.punchline: Specific insult comparing Tony's eating to barbarian warriors
jokes.explanation: Solid character-based insult with historical reference. Part of extended sequence.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: You know, it's funny. Some people eat like civilized human beings. And then there's Tony — he eats like Attila the Hun and all his relatives just showed up for a family reunion.

jokes.text: but they should serve his food in a trough.
jokes.setup: Jim already comparing Tony's eating to a Hun
jokes.punchline: Escalates to suggesting he should eat like an animal
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the eating insult with vivid imagery.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: You eat like a Hun, Tony.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: What's wrong with the way I eat?

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: What's wrong? You should eat like an animal.

jokes.text: It came from me. I'm right here. You lose!
jokes.setup: Tony breaking his own 'ignore me' rule by responding to insults
jokes.punchline: Tony declaring victory in the game he just lost
jokes.explanation: Tony's inability to stay quiet and competitive misunderstanding creates good character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: I won! I won! I finally won!

jokes.text: You're not all here, Jim.
jokes.setup: Jim saying 'let's pretend that I'm not here'
jokes.punchline: Tony's wordplay about Jim not being 'all there' mentally
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay comeback showing Tony can be witty when provoked.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Let's pretend that I'm not here.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: That shouldn't be too hard. You're not all there anyway.

jokes.text: I'd rather talk to this pretzel.
jokes.setup: Elaine asking if Tony would rather talk to Jim
jokes.punchline: Tony preferring an inanimate snack food to Jim
jokes.explanation: Visual gag with good economy. Simple but effective putdown.

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: Would you rather talk to Jim?

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: I'd rather talk to a box of crackers.

jokes.text: I mean, just because you have $3 million dollars doesn't mean you always have to pay.
jokes.setup: Discussion about who should pay for drinks
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation that Jim is extremely wealthy
jokes.explanation: Character reveal humor showing the casual way Jim's wealth is mentioned.

dialogue.speaker: Someone
dialogue.line: So who's going to pay for these drinks?

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: I'll get it.

dialogue.speaker: Someone
dialogue.line: Really? That's generous of you.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, I own three apartment buildings in Manhattan.

jokes.text: The guy sounds like a lush. How's he keep his license?
jokes.setup: Vicki saying 'I want a drink' about pregnancy news
jokes.punchline: Tony thinks she's talking about her doctor being an alcoholic
jokes.explanation: Another brilliant Tony misunderstanding showing his obliviousness to emotional context.

dialogue.speaker: Vicki
dialogue.line: I want a drink.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Your doctor drinks?

jokes.text: Oh, but if we use him, we gotta use his monkey.
jokes.setup: Tony planning wedding music with his cousin Vito on organ
jokes.punchline: Casual mention that the organ player comes with a monkey
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd detail delivered matter-of-factly. Highly quotable and memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: So Vito, you'll play the organ at my wedding?

dialogue.speaker: Vito
dialogue.line: Sure, Tony. I'll bring my monkey.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Your monkey?

dialogue.speaker: Vito
dialogue.line: Yeah, he comes with me when I play the organ.

jokes.text: And, uh, if that was a pep talk, you did just as good as Alex.
jokes.setup: Elaine's awkward, negative 'encouragement'
jokes.punchline: Tony equates her terrible pep talk with Alex's failed attempt
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows both pep talks were equally unhelpful. Good character awareness from Tony.

dialogue.speaker: Elaine
dialogue.line: You can do it, Tony. I mean, if you really put your mind to it, you might actually succeed. It's possible. Stranger things have happened.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: You know, that's the same thing Alex said to me before he tried to fly off the garage.

jokes.text: That's high tech.
jokes.setup: Someone describing Tony's apartment as elegant without being pretentious
jokes.punchline: Jim's completely different aesthetic assessment
jokes.explanation: Simple contrasting opinion joke. Shows different character perspectives.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Elegant without being pretentious? Tony's apartment looks like a Puerto Rican bordello designed by a colorblind man.

jokes.text: No, and I think I got a loose filling now over here.
jokes.setup: Alex slapping Tony to motivate him for the fight
jokes.punchline: Tony's practical concern about dental damage
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy aftermath with realistic consequence. Character-appropriate concern.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Hey, hey, hey! What are you doing?

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: I'm getting you fired up for the fight!

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Yeah, but watch the face, will ya? I got a date tonight!

jokes.text: No, and I think I got a loose filling now over here.
jokes.setup: Alex slapped Tony to motivate him
jokes.punchline: The slap caused dental damage instead of motivation
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy aftermath. The casual mention of dental damage is appropriately understated.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Ow! My tooth!

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: I was just trying to motivate you.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Well, you motivated my dentist to buy a new boat.

jokes.text: Well, I guess I'm gonna go double my bet. Is there a phone in here?
jokes.setup: Louie seeing Tony's depression before the fight
jokes.punchline: Immediately wanting to bet more money against him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie character moment - opportunistic and callous but funny.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: Hey Tony, you look a little down. You feeling okay for the fight?

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Yeah, yeah, I'm fine Louie.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: You sure? Because if you're not feeling good, I want to increase my bet against you.

jokes.text: If he's got piles, he would've told me.
jokes.setup: Leo saying Tony's problem is either pregnancy or hemorrhoids
jokes.punchline: His logic for ruling out hemorrhoids
jokes.explanation: Character-based logic about boxer-manager relationship and oversharing.

dialogue.speaker: Leo
dialogue.line: Well, it can't be hemorrhoids. Tony's been sitting down for three weeks and nothing's happened.

jokes.text: Well, it's a lousy comparison. I'm the first one to admit that. So what'd you say it for?
jokes.setup: Leo making a bad comparison then admitting it was lousy
jokes.punchline: Alex asking the obvious follow-up question
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction showing Leo's scattered thinking and Alex's exasperation.

dialogue.speaker: Leo
dialogue.line: You know, trying to fix that transmission is like trying to teach a fish to climb a tree.

dialogue.speaker: Leo
dialogue.line: Okay, that was a lousy comparison.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: Why would you even say that?

jokes.text: Well, you'll probably have more time than that because I usually get lost.
jokes.setup: Needing more than 45 seconds to resolve Tony's issues
jokes.punchline: Leo admitting he gets lost walking to the ring
jokes.explanation: Self-deprecating humor that reveals character incompetence in endearing way.

dialogue.speaker: Leo
dialogue.line: I get lost walking to the ring.

jokes.text: I insist that all my fighters have short introductions. Why? 'Cause I have to write them.
jokes.setup: Leo explaining his policy on fighter introductions
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's because he's lazy/bad at writing
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline revealing character's practical limitations with good structure.

dialogue.speaker: Leo
dialogue.line: I have a strict policy: I never do fighter introductions. You know why? Because I'm lazy and I can't write.

jokes.text: Oh, well, I'm just blowing air. Listen, I'll be managing when I'm 100.
jokes.setup: Leo saying he's retiring after this fight
jokes.punchline: Immediately taking it back with obvious exaggeration
jokes.explanation: Character moment showing Leo's inability to commit to dramatic gestures.

dialogue.speaker: Leo
dialogue.line: I'm retiring after this fight.

dialogue.speaker: Leo
dialogue.line: Well, maybe not retiring. I'll do one more fight. Okay, two more fights. Alright, I'll fight until I drop.

jokes.text: Three, four, five... I see what you mean. He looks better already.
jokes.setup: Alex saying Tony will snap out of depression when hit
jokes.punchline: Leo counting Tony getting knocked down, sarcastically agreeing
jokes.explanation: Great sarcastic response to Tony getting knocked down. Good timing and irony.

dialogue.speaker: Leo
dialogue.line: Yeah, yeah, that's right. One... two... three... Hey Tony, you feeling better yet?

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Get away from me!

dialogue.speaker: Leo
dialogue.line: Four... five... This is working, I can tell.

jokes.text: Hey, Leo, you know what's wrong with you? You're a crummy manager!
jokes.setup: Tony's anger building throughout the conversation
jokes.punchline: His final explosive declaration about Leo
jokes.explanation: Good escalation moment that gives Tony emotional breakthrough for the fight.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: His final explosive declaration about Leo

jokes.text: Tony, Vicki called me 100 times and I forgot to tell you! Go, Tony!
jokes.setup: Leo realizing Tony's anger is helping him fight
jokes.punchline: Leo deliberately making Tony angrier with exaggeration
jokes.explanation: Clever character moment where Leo turns his incompetence into strategy.

dialogue.speaker: Leo
dialogue.line: You know, Tony, your anger is really helping me fight better. I'm more aggressive, faster...

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Yeah? Well good!

dialogue.speaker: Leo
dialogue.line: In fact, I think I should make you even angrier. Your mother was a hamster and your father smelt of elderberries!

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Why you—!

dialogue.speaker: Leo
dialogue.line: And your taxi is the ugliest car on the road!

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: That's it!

jokes.text: Because it's hard watching the guy you love being force-fed his own nose.
jokes.setup: Vicki explaining why she didn't sit ringside
jokes.punchline: Graphic description of boxing violence with romantic context
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy combining romance with vivid boxing violence. Highly quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Vicki
dialogue.line: I didn't sit ringside because I didn't want to see Bobby get hurt. But then I realized I was being selfish—he wanted me there to support him. So I went to the fight, sat ringside, and watched this huge guy pummel Bobby for twelve rounds. Blood everywhere, his face swelling up, teeth flying. It was horrible. But you know what? Seeing him suffer like that for me... it was actually kind of romantic.

jokes.text: That's the thing in the middle of your face he was trying to push to the back of your head.
jokes.setup: Tony asking about the 'nose' reference
jokes.punchline: Vicki's detailed explanation of the boxing violence
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the violence description with vivid imagery.

dialogue.speaker: Vicki
dialogue.line: He got hit in the nose so many times, his nose is all spread out across his face. So when someone says 'he's got a nose on him,' they mean he's been in a lot of fights.

jokes.text: That's supposed to be a proposal? In most countries, it is.
jokes.setup: Vicki criticizing Tony's proposal attempt
jokes.punchline: Tony's defense based on international standards
jokes.explanation: Good character humor showing Tony's practical but unromantic approach.

dialogue.speaker: Vicki
dialogue.line: Tony, that was the worst proposal I've ever heard. You didn't even get down on one knee!

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Hey, in Italy, we don't need to get down on one knee. That's an American thing.

dialogue.speaker: Vicki
dialogue.line: Well, we're in America, Tony.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: So? I'm still Italian. I proposed to you the Italian way.

jokes.text: we have a split decision!
jokes.setup: Boxing match announcement overlapping with Tony and Vicki's relationship
jokes.punchline: The term applies to both the fight and their romantic situation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant structural joke that perfectly parallels the boxing and romantic storylines.

dialogue.speaker: Announcer
dialogue.line: In this corner, weighing 185 pounds, the heavyweight champion!

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: Vicki, we need to talk about us.

dialogue.speaker: Vicki
dialogue.line: What is there to talk about?

dialogue.speaker: Announcer
dialogue.line: Ladies and gentlemen, what we have here is a knockout!

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: I mean, this relationship... it's a real slugfest.

dialogue.speaker: Vicki
dialogue.line: Tell me something I don't know.

dialogue.speaker: Announcer
dialogue.line: And the winner of this evening's bout... is anybody's guess!

jokes.text: For a minute, I thought Gentry won. He did, but Tony's a good loser.
jokes.setup: Confusion about who won the fight
jokes.punchline: Clarification that Tony lost but is handling it well
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection about the fight outcome with character observation.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: So who won the fight?

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: Tony lost.

dialogue.speaker: Louie
dialogue.line: How's he taking it?

dialogue.speaker: Bobby
dialogue.line: He's handling it well. He's in the hospital, but he's in good spirits.

jokes.text: Good? He's great!
jokes.setup: Someone saying Tony is a good loser
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic escalation about Tony's losing abilities
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation joke with backhanded compliment about Tony.

dialogue.speaker: Lou
dialogue.line: Tony is a good loser.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: What do you mean I'm a good loser? I'm not a good loser, I'm a great loser! I'm the best loser in this city! Nobody loses like me! I lose with style, I lose with dignity, I lose with grace!

jokes.text: Hey, Alex, how do you know? How do you know when the woman comes along that you want to love for the rest of your life?
jokes.setup: Tony reflecting on his relationship with Vicki
jokes.punchline: Tony's sincere but confused question about love
jokes.explanation: Character moment showing Tony's emotional growth and confusion about love.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: You know, Vicki, I've been thinking about us, about what we have. I mean, is this love? Because if it is, nobody ever explained it to me very well.

jokes.text: Well, that's a question for a wiser man than I. Hmm, you're right. I'll go see if I can catch up to Leo.
jokes.setup: Alex saying the love question is for a wiser man
jokes.punchline: Tony deciding to ask Leo, the incompetent manager
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending joke that callbacks to Leo's incompetence while showing Tony's continued obliviousness.

dialogue.speaker: Alex
dialogue.line: That's a question for a wiser man than me.

dialogue.speaker: Tony
dialogue.line: I'll ask Leo.

tmdb_overview: News of his girlfriend's pregnancy floors Tony on the eve of his fight with a contender.
insight: Tony's paternal anxiety drives 43 jokes across setup-punchlines, but emotional weight undercuts comedic momentum.
